title: IMPACT purification of native proteins

before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"5k4m8","text":"    Make sure that the desired protein has the correct restriction sites and is in frame with the intein in the purification vector.
   If that is not the case natively, design primers using the sequences in the following table.
 
 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2at48","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"dcebc","text":"
 Then perform a Phusion PCR with the designed primers
  
 
 
 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E8F22B85C3B4410C951A669532F8816D","height":"347","is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"Screen Shot 2016-11-25 at 13.21.46.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/gs2e7qw.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/gs2e7qw.png","width":"566"}}}}
steps: Digest the pPCR product with NdeI & SapI in minimum 2 hours.
Transform the minipreped plasmid from step 7 into E. Coli :ER2566
Take 10 ml Chitin beads put into a 50ml falcon tube undetermined
Open valve and drip eluate into tubes in 0.5 ml aliquots
Purify the digest on colums
Ligate the purified digest products using the preferred ligation protocol
Make a TSB transformation into E. Coli :K-12 use your preferred transformation protocol
Inspect the ligation with a colonyPCR
Make ON of the verified transformation
Purify the plasmid from the ON from step 1.7 using Miniprep
Inoculate 1L LB+Amp with a fresh colony from the transformation above.
Incubate at 37ºC till it reaches a OD 600 at 0.5-0.8
Add IPTG for a end concentration of 0.4 mM
Incubate at 16-20ºC overnight.
Centrifuge the 1L ON at 5000G at 4ºC - remove the supernatant. 00:15:00
Resuspend the cellpellet in 100 ml cold column buffer - (See recipe in description) undetermined
Centrifuge at 8000G at 2ºC - remove the supernatant. 00:20:00
Resuspend the cellpellet in a small volume of cold column buffer (2-4 mL)
Lyse the cells using french press
Centrifuge the lysant at 15000G at 4ºC 00:30:00
Centrifuge at 200G - remove the supernantant 00:05:00
Wash with 50 ml Column Buffer undetermined
Centrifuge at 200G - remove the supernantant 00:05:00
Load the supernantant from step 18
Attach on a rotor with slow spin 02:00:00
Add to a collum, open valve just enough that it slowly drips out.
Add column Buffer. undetermined
When the column buffer has run trough, close the valve and add Cleavage Buffer (just enough that i covers the beads + 1ml.) (See recipe in description)
leave at room temperature over night (minimum 16 hours)
Measure protein concentration
The aliquots with the highest concentrations is mixed and used for further work.
creator: Brian K Baltzar
